title: Inviting Light Australian Vegetable Tagine
description: A yeasty and light Australian dish featuring olive oil and tagine-cooked vegetables, perfect for a brunch mood, and is Jain-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, yeast mixture, and a pinch of salt.
In a separate bowl, toss the onion, garlic, zucchini, bell pepper, and cherry tomatoes with a pinch of salt.
Divide the vegetable mixture among 4 individual mini cocottes.
Drizzle the yeast and oil mixture over the vegetables in each cocotte.
Cover the cocottes with lids and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the lids and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Remove the cocottes from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
